name: @vanillagreen/pi-skills-manager
description: Polished Pi skills manager with a dedicated /skill menu for browsing, previewing, inserting, creating, editing, renaming, deleting, and toggling skills while preserving native /skill:name invocation.

settings.label: Enable skills manager
settings.description: Register the /skill manager view, startup skill-list hiding, and skills management UI. If disabled, /skill enable remains available as a recovery command. Native /skill:name commands stay controlled by Pi's Skill commands setting.

settings.label: Glyph style
settings.description: Glyphs for vstack-owned UI chrome. Unicode is the default; ASCII uses plain box/status/separator characters for terminal compatibility. pi-tool-renderer's global override wins when set.

settings.label: Hide startup skills block
settings.description: Hide Pi's built-in startup [Skills] list so skills are managed through the dedicated menu instead.

settings.label: AI skill generation
settings.description: Use the currently selected model/thinking level to draft new SKILL.md files. If unavailable or disabled, a deterministic fallback template is used.

settings.label: Default create location
settings.description: Initial visibility choice when creating a skill.

settings.label: Popup width
settings.description: Preferred overlay width. Accepts a number of columns or a percentage string such as 82%.

settings.label: Popup max height
settings.description: Maximum overlay height. Accepts a number of rows or a percentage string.

settings.label: Visible list rows
settings.description: Maximum list rows shown before scrolling in browse mode. Short terminals shrink this to keep popup controls visible.

author: vanillagreen
